author: nflaig
content: can you elaborate a bit on that Enrico, where do you see this problem?

author: nflaig
content: is it regarding `prepare_execution_payload` in slot N-1 if you are the proposer of slot N?

author: potuz
content: I think this is about deciding whether to do this at N or N-1

author: nflaig
content: there's no way you do that at slot N? I mean we need to send FCU in hte previous slot, and also emit SSE event for builders with correct withdrawals

author: potuz
content: Oh crap FCU is really the problem! This is really a problem cause you can't decide your head until the next slot and you've counted attestations

author: potuz
content: I guess we're going to have to relax that?

author: potuz
content: I guess it's fine, just as today we will be forced to compute attributes and do the state transition for that, then send FCU and it head changes in the start of the slot send a new FCU

author: terencechain
content:
yes, this is what im dealing with now, there's two places typically, in prysm case
- we compute payload attribute and call FCU at n-1
- if there's a payload attribute cache miss (head changes last min), we compute payload attribute and call FCU at n right before proposing

author: nflaig
content: same in lodestar, I don't think this will change in gloas though

author: nflaig
content:
> we compute payload attribute and call FCU at n-1
timing of this will change, since payload might be at late as 9 seconds or so, I am not even sure yet what signal we should use to send the correct FCU, it seems to me that counting PTC votes is the right approach

author: potuz
content: PTC is a bad thing to do I believe

author: potuz
content: As of now, if you synced the Payload of your head it becomes your head

author: potuz
content: There's some argument to be made about DA and not being a supernode, but could never made Francesco agree with it yet

author: terencechain
content:
depends on client implementation, whats interesting is you may not need to copy the state in `n-1` case because you wont need to save the post state anyway, the state is "thrown away"

and in `n` case i dont mind the copy because it's just a rare case to hit
